{{Infobox book
| name = Of Shadow and Sea
| slug = ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea
| precedes = [[Of Dawn and Darkness (novel)|Of Dawn and Darkness]]
| follows = [[Of Sea and Shadow (novel)|Of Sea and Shadow]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea.jpg|250px]]
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|Fifteen years ago, young [[Shera]] and [[Mari]] were child assassins for [[Maxwell]]'s gang. After [[Maxwell]] executed [[Mari]], [[Shera]] killed him, leading to her being discovered and taken in by the [[Consultant]] [[Kerian]]. Recognizing her potential after she confessed to killing the notorious [[Rudeus Maxwell]], [[Kerian]] brought [[Shera]] to [[Gray Island]] to train her as a professional [[Consultant]]. During this time, [[Shera]], [[Meia]], and [[Lucan]] were selected by [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] of the [[Aurelian Empire]] to undergo rigorous training as '[[Gardeners]],' a secret order of assassins. The Emperor revealed that his immortality was tied to the '[[Dead Mother]],' a beating [[Heart of Nakothi]], and tasked the trio with the ultimate mission: to kill him when the deity [[Nakothi]] eventually rises to take control of him.

In the present day, [[Shera]] is an established [[Consultant]]. Her life becomes complicated when she is tasked by High [[Council]] [[Architects]] [[Kerian]] and [[Yala]] to assassinate [[Naberius Clayborn]], a mission involving the [[Heart of Nakothi]]—an object the [[Blackwatch Guild]] intends to use to name a new Emperor. [[Shera]] infiltrates Clayborn's ship, *[[The Testament]]*, but the mission goes awry during a violent confrontation with the [[Soulbound]] Jyrine Tessella Marten{{!}}Jyrine Marten and Clayborn himself. Retreating to [[Gray Island]] to recover, [[Shera]] learns that [[Meia]] has undergone painful alchemical enhancements to keep pace with her. 

The conflict escalates when [[Shera]] and [[Meia]] intercept the [[Blackwatch]] on [[Nakothi]]’s [[Island]] to secure the Heart. After a chaotic battle involving the [[Champion]] [[Urzaia Woodsman]] and monstrous [[Children of Nakothi]], [[Shera]] successfully seizes the sentient Heart. However, upon returning to [[Gray Island]], the artifact's power proves uncontrollable, shattering its restraints and releasing the dead. Tensions rise within the [[Guild]] as Yala the High Mason{{!}}High Councilor Yala attempts to seize the Heart for herself, while [[Lucan]] warns that the [[Sleepless cult]] intends to use the artifact to trigger a catastrophe.

As political decay settles over The Empire{{!}}the Empire, [[Calder Marten]] arrives on the island wearing [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]]'s crown to claim the Heart. In the ensuing chaos, [[Lucan]] uses his 'Reader' abilities to awaken Shera's bronze knife{{!}}Shera’s knife into a sentient weapon named [[Syphren]]. A massive [[Handmaiden of the Dead Mother]] emerges from a rift, devastating [[Gray Island]] and bringing an entourage of monsters. Amidst this invasion, [[Shera]] engages in a multi-sided battle against [[Calder Marten]] and [[Urzaia Woodsman]]. Using her new blade, [[Shera]] manages to kill [[Urzaia]] and eventually plunges [[Syphren]] into the [[Handmaiden]]'s head, defeating the creature in an explosion of emerald light.

In the aftermath, Bastion's Veil{{!}}Bastion’s Veil vanishes, leaving the island exposed. [[Shera]] is informed by the alchemist [[Nathanael Bareius]] that a massive guild war is imminent between those seeking to restore The Empire{{!}}the Empire and those wishing to move beyond it. As [[Meia]] attempts to escape captivity on [[Calder Marten]]'s ship, which is heading toward the Capital, [[Shera]] receives a ransom note threatening [[Meia]]'s life, setting the stage for a final confrontation.}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|Fifteen years ago, young [[Shera]] and [[Mari]] successfully assassinated a target named [[Kamba Nomen]] as part of [[Maxwell]]'s gang of child assassins. Upon returning to their safe house, they discovered the group was under attack by [[Consultants]] sent to retrieve the children. After [[Maxwell]] executed [[Mari]] for being a liability, [[Shera]] retaliated by killing him, leading to her being discovered and collected by the [[Consultant]] [[Kerian]].}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Shera]], a bored [[Consultant]] working in a quiet chapter house with her partner [[Ayana]], accepts a desperate contract from [[Ulrich Fletcher]] to reclaim his alchemical workshop from criminals. [[Shera]] tracks the gang to the warehouse, where she observes them terrorizing a man named [[Tombstone]]. Disguised in black, [[Shera]] intervenes by disarming and threatening the leader, [[Wellin]], before entering the [[workshop]] to finish the job.}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|After the death of the notorious [[Gladstone Kidnapper]], [[Rudeus Maxwell]], a young girl named [[Shera]] confesses to [[Kerian]], a [[Consultant]], that she was the one who killed him. Recognizing the girl's potential and unusual composure, [[Kerian]] decides to take [[Shera]] under her wing and brings her to the [[Gray Island]]. Upon arriving at the mysterious, fog-shrouded island, [[Kerian]] begins training [[Shera]] in the professional mannerisms required to become a [[Consultant]].}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|After completing a mission to neutralize a gang in an alchemist's [[workshop]], [[Shera]] receives a coded summons from [[Yala]] to the [[Gray Island]]. There, she meets with High [[Council]] [[Architects]] [[Kerian]] and [[Yala]] to discuss a mission involving the [[Heart of Nakothi]], an object the [[Blackwatch Guild]] intends to use to name a new Emperor. [[Shera]] is presented with the task of assassinating [[Naberius Clayborn]], a Chronicler traveling by ship, and she requests twenty-four hours to decide how to proceed with the high-stakes assignment.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|After arriving on [[Gray Island]], [[Shera]] is led by the [[Consultant]] [[Kerian]] through a series of hidden structures and training grounds designed to maintain the [[Guild]]'s mystery. They visit [[Zhen]], an alchemist and former instructor, who tests [[Shera]]'s wit and social intuition through a series of verbal challenges and a meal. The chapter concludes with [[Shera]] being tricked into attacking a man who is actually a fellow member, a test used by the [[Consultants]] to evaluate her psychological state before moving her to the Garden.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Shera]] visits [[Lucan]], a powerful Reader held in a high-status prison, to discuss her upcoming mission to assassinate [[Naberius Clayborn]] and destroy the [[Heart of Nakothi]]. After securing a deal with [[Yala]] to release [[Lucan]] if she succeeds, [[Shera]] infiltrates the ship *[[The Testament]]* by swimming under its hull and neutralizing a [[Soulbound]] crew member. However, her mission goes awry when she enters the wrong cabin and is discovered by Jyrine Tessella Marten{{!}}Jyrine Marten, who reveals unexpected [[Soulbound powers]].}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|Thirteen years ago on [[Gray Island]], three students—[[Shera]], [[Meia]], and [[Lucan]]—undergo rigorous training as [[Gardeners]], a secret order of assassins. After demonstrating their skills in a simulated assassination task, the students are prepared for an unexpected visit from [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] of the [[Aurelian Empire]]. The Emperor arrives via a massive [[Kameira]] alongside [[General Jarelys Teach]] to personally meet the three trainees.}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|During a confrontation on the ship *[[The Testament]]*, [[Shera]] engages in a violent battle with the [[Soulbound]] [[Jyrine Tessella Marten]] and later neutralizes Calder Marten{{!}}Captain Calder [[Marten]], but she is ultimately forced to retreat after being overwhelmed by [[Naberius Clayborn]] and his allies. [[Shera]] returns to the [[Gray Island]] to recover from severe burns in the infirmary, where she is visited by her friend [[Meia]]. The chapter concludes with [[Meia]] reflecting on the painful alchemical enhancements she underwent to surpass [[Shera]], as both women prepare for an upcoming mission in five days.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|Thirteen years in the past, trainees [[Shera]], [[Meia]], and [[Lucan]] debate the morality of their profession while cleaning their training grounds. Their discussion is interrupted by the sudden arrival of [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]], who uses alchemy to create a path toward their tower and commands them to jump. After [[Shera]] falls from the window, [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] announces that he is taking the three trainees to train under him personally.}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Shera]] and a specialized team of [[Consultants]] depart on the Bastion's Shadow{{!}}Bastion’s Shadow, a secret Navigator’s ship{{!}}Navigator ship built with Elder parts, to intercept the [[Blackwatch]] on [[Nakothi]]’s [[Island]]. After navigating through a Deepstriders{{!}}Deepstrider migration and the predatory [[Lhirin Island]], they arrive to find the [[Blackwatch]] nearing the retrieval of a [[Heart of Nakothi]]. [[Shera]] initiates an ambush by signaling her team to attack, catching the [[Blackwatch]] and [[Calder Marten]]'s crew off guard.}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|During a chaotic battle in a crater, [[Shera]] and [[Meia]] fight through [[Watchmen]], the [[Champion]] [[Urzaia Woodsman]], and a swarm of monstrous [[Children of Nakothi]] to secure the Heart. After [[Meia]] engages [[Urzaia]] in combat, [[Shera]] manages to wound [[Calder Marten]] and seize the Heart from him. The two [[Consultants]] successfully escape the battlefield with heavy casualties, heading toward their ship with the pulsing, sentient Heart in [[Shera]]'s possession.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|Thirteen years ago, [[Shera]] appears before the [[Council of Architects]] to be formally inducted into the [[Guild of Am’haranai]] at [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]]'s request. Despite her skepticism toward [[Yala the High Mason]]'s warnings, [[Shera]] completes a magical oath using a mystical vessel filled with blue fog. After swearing her loyalty, she is presented with a belt of ancient bronze blades, officially beginning her service as a [[Gardener]].}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|During a perilous voyage to the [[Gray Island]], [[Shera]] and [[Meia]] fend off multiple assassination attempts by [[Consultants]] influenced by the [[Great Elder]] [[Nakothi]]'s whispers. Upon arrival, a confrontation erupts between [[Shera]] and Yala the High Mason{{!}}High Councilor Yala, who intends to keep the Heart rather than destroy it. The group retreats to a secure site to contain the artifact, but the Heart's power proves too great, shattering its stone restraints and releasing the dead from nearby crypts.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Twelve]] years ago, [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] trains [[Lucan]] in 'Active Reading{{!}}active Reading,' demonstrating how to physically alter an object's structure by understanding its fundamental nature. In a separate training session, [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] pushes [[Meia]] to her physical limits during a hunt against a [[Duskwinder]], ultimately deciding to provide her with a new body. Finally, [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] oversees [[Shera]]'s combat training, where she successfully kills several prisoners to prove her potential as an assassin.}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Meia]] and [[Yala]] engage in a tense confrontation regarding the [[Guild]]'s values and [[Shera]]'s perceived treachery, ending with [[Meia]] locking her mother in a reinforced closet. Meanwhile, [[Shera]] and her companions battle resurrected [[Consultants]] in a crypt until [[Meia]] arrives to secure the [[Heart of Nakothi]]. [[Shera]] then brings the artifact to [[Lucan]], who warns that the [[Sleepless cult]] intends for the Heart to be destroyed to trigger a catastrophic event, prompting them to seek a new way to contain its power.}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|Ten years ago, the three [[Gardeners]]—[[Shera]], [[Meia]], and [[Lucan]]—successfully completed their first official mission by infiltrating a castle and killing the mad alchemist [[Albadol Crane]]. Following the mission, [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] summons them to reveal a grave secret regarding his immortality. He discloses that he survives by using a beating [[Heart of Nakothi]], the [[Dead Mother]], which he keeps in a pewter shell.}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|As [[Shera]] and [[Meia]] navigate the political decay of The Empire{{!}}the Empire, [[Kerian]] warns them that [[Calder Marten]]'s ship is approaching the island to potentially claim Heart of Nakothi{{!}}Nakothi’s Heart. [[Shera]] struggles with her own weapons' growing sentience and has a tense exchange with the captive [[Jyrine]], who reveals the [[Sleepless]]'s goal of communicating with [[Great Elders]]. The situation escalates when [[Calder Marten]] arrives wearing [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]]'s crown, using its Authority{{!}}authority to command the [[Consultants]]; however, [[Lucan]] facilitates [[Shera]]'s escape by throwing her the box containing the Heart.}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|The Emperor reveals to the three [[Gardeners]] that his immortality comes from a [[Great Elder]] and informs them of a [[Blackwatch]] thief attempting to steal another of [[Nakothi]]'s hearts. In preparation for an urgent mission to intercept the thief, [[Shera]] and [[Lucan]] gear up, leading to a tense emotional confrontation where [[Lucan]] expresses his fear of [[Shera]]'s cold nature and his commitment to her survival. The chapter concludes with [[Shera]] leaving [[Lucan]] alone as he prepares to invest his powers into her equipment for the upcoming battle.}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|While imprisoned, [[Lucan]] uses his Reader abilities to undergo the dangerous process of [[Awakening]] Shera's bronze knife{{!}}Shera's knife, successfully transforming it into a powerful sentient weapon named [[Syphren]]. Meanwhile, [[Shera]] escapes a [[labyrinth]] only to be attacked by [[Consultants]] under the [[Emperor's command]], leading to a violent confrontation where she accidentally kills a young woman and loses the [[Heart of Nakothi]]. Elsewhere, [[Meia]] engages in a strategic duel against the [[Champion]] [[Urzaia Woodsman]] in an ancient underground arena.}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|After [[Lucan]] uses his power to tap into the ancient, layered [[Intent]] of a bronze knife, the group travels via a massive sea serpent called a Deepstriders{{!}}Deepstrider to a grotesque island made of flesh and bone. Upon arriving, [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] reveals his mission is to hunt a nameless traitor who possesses secrets regarding his youth and the [[Dead Mother]]. The chapter concludes with the group sprinting toward a gathering of men at a mausoleum to engage their target.}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|A titanic [[Handmaiden of the Dead Mother]] emerges from a rift on [[Gray Island]], bringing an entourage of monstrous [[Children of Nakothi]] that devastate the landscape. [[Shera]] narrowly escapes the creatures by hiding in an underground arena, while [[Lucan]] manages to escape his cell using a sentient knife and begins searching for her. Meanwhile, [[Meia]] continues a ferocious, instinct-driven duel with [[Urzaia]] amidst the chaos of the monster invasion.}}
* '''Chapter 22:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|Ten years ago, [[Shera]] and her companions quickly dispatch a group of gunmen before descending into a wound in the island's flesh to find [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]]. Inside a cavernous chamber filled with red, tendon-like wires, [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] interrogates and then magically incapacitates a [[Blackwatch traitor]] who warns of something awakening. The chapter concludes with [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] standing before a large, pulsing heart at the center of the island's anatomy, declaring his intent to bring about rebirth before plunging the room into darkness.}}
* '''Chapter 23:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|After defeating a [[Child of Nakothi]], [[Kerian]] accesses an ancient vessel in the Council chambers{{!}}Council chamber and discovers through a vision that attempting to use the [[Veil]]'s power would allow the [[Handmaiden]] to break it entirely. Meanwhile, in the arena, a chaotic battle erupts between [[Shera]], [[Calder Marten]], [[Meia]], and [[Urzaia]]. The conflict reaches a turning point when [[Shera]] uses an Awakened blade to kill [[Urzaia]], only for the Handmaidens of Nakothi{{!}}Handmaiden of Nakothi to tear off the arena's roof.}}
* '''Chapter 24:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|Ten years ago, [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] of the [[Aurelian Empire]] leads [[Lucan]], [[Meia]], and [[Shera]] into a cavern where he appears to succumb to madness and attacks his own companions. After a violent confrontation that leaves [[Lucan]] injured, [[Emperor (Elder Empire)|the Emperor]] reveals the battle was a calculated training exercise designed to test their capabilities. He explains that he has made a bargain with the deity [[Nakothi]] and tasks the trio with one ultimate mission: to kill him when she eventually rises to take control of him.}}
* '''Chapter 25:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|As the [[Handmaiden]] and her [[Children of Nakothi]] attack, [[Shera]] discovers that her new blade, [[Syphren]], allows her to absorb the power of fallen enemies, leading [[Lucan]] to suspect she is [[Soulbound]]. During the chaotic battle, [[Meia]] throws [[Shera]] toward the massive Elder, where [[Shera]] manages to plunge her blade into the creature's head. The resulting explosion of emerald light defeats the [[Handmaiden]], leaving a revitalized but eventually exhausted [[Shera]] to be carried away by her companions.}}
* '''Chapter 26:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|After [[Meia]] attempts to apprehend the escaped [[Navigator]] [[Calder Marten]], she is ambushed and knocked unconscious. Meanwhile, [[Shera]] recovers in the infirmary and learns that Bastion's Veil{{!}}Bastion’s Veil has vanished, leaving the island exposed but free of [[Elderspawn]]. [[Shera]] is then summoned to a meeting with the High [[Council]] and their client, the alchemist [[Nathanael Bareius]], who reveals that a massive guild war is imminent between those seeking to restore The Empire{{!}}the Empire and those wishing to move beyond it.}}
* '''Chapter 27:''' {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|Ten years ago, [[Kerian]] reveals to [[Shera]] that the Watchman's crew sought to harvest a heart from [[Nakothi]]'s corpse for eternal life and provides information regarding a group called 'Sleepless{{!}}The Sleepless.' In the present day, [[Meia]] escapes her restraints on [[Calder Marten]]'s ship after being captured and learns the vessel is heading for the Capital. Meanwhile, [[Shera]] receives a ransom note from [[Calder]] threatening [[Meia]]'s life, but she decides to let [[Meia]] attempt to rescue herself.}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Shera]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Meia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Lucan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Emperor (Elder Empire)|Emperor]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Nakothi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Kerian]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Calder Marten]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Elderspawn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Yala]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Maxwell]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Dead Mother]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Ayana]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Reader (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Urzaia Woodsman]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Handmaiden]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Naberius Clayborn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Tristania]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Jyrine]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Zhen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Mari]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Tyril]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Albadol Crane]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Navigator]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Urzaia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Nightwyrm (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Calder]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Architect]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Duskwinder]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Emperor (Elder Empire)|The Emperor]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Jyrine Tessella Marten]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Kelarac]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Silent One]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Naberius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Kthanikahr]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Jorin Curse-breaker]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Chronicler (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Captain (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Gray Island]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Consultants]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Shepherds (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Architects]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Gardener]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Gardeners]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Am’haranai]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Consultant]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Mason]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Blackwatch]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Masons]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Capital (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Bastion's Veil]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Aion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Consultant's Guild]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Council of Architects]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Imperial Guard]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Alchemist's Guild]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Kanatalia]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Regents]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Sleepless]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Izyrian]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Readers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Island]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Witnesses (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Elders]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Greenwardens]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Great Elders]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Empire (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Shepherd (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Blackwatch Guild]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Watchmen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Aurelian Empire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Children]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Heartland]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Handmaiden of the Dead Mother]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[High Councilors (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Garden (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Reading]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Nakothi's song]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Heart of Nakothi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Heart (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Syphren]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Sandborn Hydra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Shera's shears]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Bastion's Shadow]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[The Testament]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Syphren, the Whispering Death]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Shera's shear]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Emperor's crown (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Kameira]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Intent]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Soulbound]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Children of Nakothi]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Deepstriders]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Awakening]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Children of the Dead Mother]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Waveriders]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Great Elder]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Heartlander]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Champion]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Othaghor]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Awakened objects]]}} • {{spoiler|book=ee-shadow-01-of-shadow-and-sea|[[Stormwing (The Elder Empire: Shadow)]]}}

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:Of Shadow and Sea]]
